title: CD-2 Harvester
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:CD2Harvester1-FF31.png]] |name=CD-2 Harvester |creator= |manufacturer=[[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]]''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'' |designer= |homeworld= |line= |model= |class=[[Agrirobot/Legends|Agrirobot]]''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]'' |degree= |cost=45,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |length= |width= |height=20 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]{{FactFile|31|HAR 1-2|CD-2 Harvester Droid}} |mass= |gender= |sensor=Limited Visual sensor suit |plating=Bright [[Color/Legends|yellow]]''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |armament= |equipment=Harvesting blades, tiller arms |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|For a machine it's pretty dumb.|Blue Max comments on the CD-2's intelligence|}} '''CD-2 Harvesters''' were a type of [[Agrirobot/Legends|agrirobot]] produced by the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], the controlling body of the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. Not intelligent [[Droid/Legends|droids]], they followed pre-programmed routes through fields of crops. They were capable of carrying out a range of agricultural functions including sowing seeds, harvesting crops and treating soil. Used throughout the Corporate [[Sector/Legends|Sector]] and by various other [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] factions, CD-2s were considered one of the best agribots available. A CD-2 was used by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Gender/Legends|his]] comrades to escape from the [[Security Police/Legends|Security Police]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Orron III]] in around [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. Solo's [[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Blue Max]] took control of the agribot and used it to transport the group, before sending it to plow through a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], where it was destroyed. ==Description== {{Quote|Afoot, Captain Solo…but the harvester has insulated deck plates!|The academic Rekkon explains to Han Solo how they can survive driving through an electric fence|[[Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)|''Han Solo at Stars' End'' comic]]}} CD-2 Harvesters were gigantic automated [[agrirobot]]s which were bright [[Color/Legends|yellow]] in color. The machines were equipped with a large set of cyclonic [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] harvesting blades, which were positioned on arms in a twenty [[Meter/Legends|meter]] row at the front of the [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. Directly behind the blades was a large intake shaft where the reaped produce would be collected, cleaned, separated and then be passed through a discharge chute and stored in an internal tandem load-carrier. Chaff and other unneeded material was released through a discharge chute at the rear of the vehicle, above which an external hydraulic storage hopper was located when retracted. As well as their harvesting equipment, the CD-2s had three large hydraulic soil-tilling arms positioned at their front, which when raised and not in use were positioned above the harvest blades. Also located on the front of the droid was an area where additional nose attachments could be equipped to the droid which allowed for additional agricultural processes to be carried out. [[File:CD2Harvester2-FF31.png|thumb|left|250px|The rear view of a CD-2 Harvester]] A CD-2's control center, which consisted of various hook-ups and components, was located at the top of its chassis, which was twenty meters high. The control center was protected by a heavy and durable industrial cover, although a member of the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Species/Legends|species]] was capable of ripping this off with tremendous effort. CD-2's had primitive guidance systems and were relatively clumsy and slow moving on their four tractor treaded tires, the front two of which were smaller in size. The droids had a speed governor to limit how fast they could go, but if the governor was disabled, the vehicle could travel at up to forty [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]]. The harvesters traveled pre-programmed routes, but could also be managed by a remote interface control mechanism. The agrirobots had a limited optical perception suit located on the front side of their chassis, although this could only be used to monitor condition of grain directly in front of them. It could not detect obstacles like organic workers or react well to sudden changes, and although the machine could be remotely shut down in emergencies accidents did sometimes occur. Various programs could be installed onto the CD-2s to allow them to perform additional agricultural tasks, so they could be adapted to the different seasons they worked in. Several maintenance ladders were attached to the sides of the droid and allowed it to be mounted from ground level, with the ladders leading all the way up to the control center. Narrow catwalks with hand rails were also constructed across the droid to allow movement over it, and these included thick insulated strips large enough for a [[Human/Legends|Human]] to stand entirely within. Generally uncomplicated machines, they were extremely difficult to damage with small arms fire, which could remove cutter blades and non-vital plating but to little effect. The droids could be destroyed by larger weaponry, however, such as [[cannonade]]s. The machine was kept running by a power plant, which could, under heavy fire, explode with enough force to tear the entire machine in two. ==Role== CD-2s were designed to go beyond assisting agricultural industry and complete the entire labor process themselves, from sowing and to harvesting. They were used to harvest the huge agricultural fields of produce on [[Planet/Legends|planets]] like [[Orron III]]. They would follow their pre-programmed route through the fields collecting the grain and requiring no organic crew to operate them on site. The harvester was also capable of soil preparation, crop planting and yield expansion, using its tilling arms. This could create optimal seedbed aeration, moisture saturation and allowed for improved root penetration. With different nose attachments the agrirobots could also distribute liquid fertiliser or granule herbicide pellets and act as a furrower. Many of the top one hundred most commonly used crops, including [[tallgrain]] and [[Denta bean/Legends|denta beans]], were harvested by CD-2s. Some specialist crops, such as berries, could not be harvested by the CD-2, however. The droids were especially useful for struggling farmers on desolate worlds, as the droid could function in any terrain, and its tillers could break even the hardest soil. ==History== {{Quote|Can you program this crate so it'll run without you?''"''
''"''That's what it's built to do, but it'll remember only simple things, Captain.|Solo asks Blue Max to program a CD-2|}} The CD-2 was manufactured by the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], the controlling body of the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. Considered one of the best agricultural droids available, the harvesters were sold for 45,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] The droid was used on many worlds within the Corporate Sector, but thousands of units were also purchased and used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], as well as various planetary confederacies. The droids were vital to the success of agricultural worlds such as [[Taanab/Legends|Taanab]], [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]] and [[Salliche/Legends|Salliche]], which not be able to sustain their vast agricultural output without them. Due to their efficiency and importance, the droids continued to be used even after they became considered fairly primitive in technological standards. [[File:Chewie_on_a_CD-2.jpg|thumb|right|Chewbacca fights atop a CD-2|200px]] One CD-2 on Orron III was programmed to harvest large fields of an [[Arcon Multinode Agricorp]] hybrid grain. While the droid was routinely harvesting a field in around [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and a group of accomplices entered the area in a [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmer]] attempting to escape from the [[Security Police/Legends|Security Police]]. After having their vehicle shot down, one of the group, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], boarded the harvester and placed the [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] droid [[Blue Max]] into the CD-2's control panel. The slicer then managed to take control of agricultural behemoth and used it to run down several of the Security Police officers pursuing Solo and the others. Concentrated fire on the droid forced Blue Max to order it to flee, however, picking up the rest of the fleeing group as it did, apart from Chewbacca, who was captured by the Police. The harvester was then piloted through the electrified security fence of a nearby [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], where Solo and his allies abandoned it with orders to plow through the rest of the port. As its one-time crew fled to Solo's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], the harvester dutifully carried out its orders to run through the port, being attacked by Security Police as it did so. The droid was partially disabled but made it halfway through the port before a cannonade shot caused its power plant to explode, tearing the CD-2 in half. ==Behind the scenes== The CD-2 Harvester, identified only as harvester, first appeared in the novel ''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]'', which was written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1979]]. [[Archie Goodwin]] and [[Alfredo Alcala]] adapted the story into a newspaper [[Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)|comic strip]] published in [[1980]] which also included the harvester. The [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] adaptation included several notable differences in the scene including the harvester, with Solo and his companions all mounting the droid together instead of just Chewbacca initially. In the comic it is the academic [[Rekkon]], not Chewbacca, who opens the hatch and puts Blue Max in the control center. The comic also depicts the harvester as grey, not yellow, and with distinctly different harvesting blades. This article assumes the information from the original novel and not the adaptation is correct. [[File:Grey_CD-2.jpg|thumb|left|The depiction of the CD-2 in the comic adaptation|200px]] The agrirobot was first given its model designation of CD-2 in the [[1993]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'', which was written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and also provided role-playing stats for the droid. The droid then received an entry in [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' series in the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 31|31st issue]]. The magazine claimed the droids stood twenty meters tall as opposed to the fifteen-meter height given by the ''Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook''. This article assumes the fact file, as the more recent source, is correct. ==Appearances== *''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]'' {{1st}} *[[Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)|''Han Solo at Stars' End'' comic]] {{1stp}} ==Sources== *''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'' {{ID|Harvester Droid, Model CD-2}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' {{1stID|CD-2 Harvester}} *{{FactFile|31|HAR 1-2|CD-2 Harvester Droid}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of CD-2 Harvesters}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=CD-2-Erntedroide |nl=CD-2 Harvester |pl=Żniwiarka CD-2 }} [[Category:Agricultural droid models]] [[Category:Corporate Sector Authority products]] [[Category:Droid models of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Droid models of the New Republic]]
